name: Clifty Creek Employees
description_short: Clifty Creek Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving employees of Clifty Creek Power Plant and their families in Madison, Indiana.
description_long:
Clifty Creek Employees Credit Union operates as a federally-insured credit union based in Madison, Indiana, serving a specific employee base and their extended families. The credit union is restricted to employees of Clifty Creek Power Plant, contracted employees with six months tenure at the facility, OVEC (Ohio Valley Electric Company) employees assigned to the plant, and qualifying family members including spouses, children, grandchildren, parents, and siblings.

The credit union offers standard banking services including online banking, vehicle financing, and rate matching programs. Current auto loan rates advertised include rates as low as 5.49% APR for 2023 and newer vehicles and 6.24% APR for 2021-2022 vehicles on 5-year terms. Members have access to online banking platforms and can contact the institution via phone, email, or in-person at their single Madison location.

What distinguishes Clifty Creek is its employee-focused membership model, restricting access to a defined group tied to the power plant industry. This creates a tight-knit member base but limits accessibility compared to open-field credit unions. The organization maintains conservative communication, offering a Rate Match program but providing limited product information on their website.

The credit union appears to be a stable, modest-sized institution serving its niche membership. However, the website contains minimal detail about savings accounts, checking options, loan products beyond auto financing, or other standard banking services. The lack of comprehensive product transparency and the restricted membership field may limit usefulness for consumers outside the eligible employee categories. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking platform and account access
Auto loans (2023 and newer vehicles, 2021-2022 vehicles)
Membership eligibility for power plant employees and families
In-person banking at Madison branch location
pros: Competitive auto loan rates as low as 5.49% APR for newer vehicles (2023+)
Rate matching program available to help members secure favorable lending terms
Member-owned cooperative structure with no external shareholders
Online banking access for convenience and account management
Restricted membership ensures services tailored to power plant employee community
Established local presence with physical branch in Madison, Indiana
FDIC insurance protections through federal credit union charter
cons: Highly restricted membership eligibility tied exclusively to Clifty Creek Power Plant, making it inaccessible to general consumers
Website lacks detailed information about savings accounts, checking products, CDs, and other standard banking services
Limited product transparency—only auto loan rates are prominently displayed
Single branch location in Madison, IN with no online or remote membership options advertised
No information about minimum balances, fees, or other account terms on public website
best_for: Current employees of Clifty Creek Power Plant seeking competitive auto financing
Family members of eligible Clifty Creek or OVEC employees needing basic banking services
Power plant workers wanting member-owned financial institution benefits
diagnosis: Clifty Creek Employees Credit Union is best for current or eligible family members of Clifty Creek Power Plant employees seeking competitive auto loans and basic banking services. The primary caveat is that membership is strictly limited to this specific employee group and their families—the general public cannot join.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Clifty Creek Employees Credit Union serves employees of Clifty Creek Power Plant and families in Madison, Indiana.
